metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_1116
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Immuno EM in afferent terminals of rat DRG.

8/9/2017 Pim
- Based on the known transmembrane topology of the protein, the "integral component of ... membrane" term was chosen.
- Afferent input into DRG is known to be glutamatergic. Experimental description: All animals were treated according to the guidelines of the Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) of the University of North Carolina. Sprague-Dawley rats (200-300 g, Charles River, Raleigh, NC) were deeply anesthetized with sodium pentobarbital (60 mg/kg, i.p.) and perfused through the heart with 100 ml normal saline, containing 500U heparin sodium at room temperature, followed by either 1 or 4% paraformaldehyde (PF) in phosphate buffer (PB, 0.1 M, pH 7.4). Lumbar segments of spinal cord and L4 DRG were removed and post-fixed for 2 h at 4 8C in the fixative used for perfusion. Blocks of spinal cord were embedded in agar, cut transversely at 70 mm on a Vibratome and stored in PB at 4 8C for up to 2 days before processing. The L4 DRG were cryoprotected in 30% sucrose in PB overnight at 4 8C, embedded in Tissue-Tek O.C.T. compound (Sakura Finetek, Torrance, CA), and sectioned on a cryostat at 15 mm. Every 6th section was thaw-mounted onto pre-cleaned slides and stored at K20 8C.
